name: Sam Arnold's Boneless Rump Roast
recipeInstructions:
1. Marinate the meat for a couple of hours in a liquid that is slightly acidic--a liquid such as wine, beer, vinegar, or lemon juice.
2. Preheat oven to 325°F (165°C).
3. Roast for 15 minutes per pound at 325°F until fully cooked. For example, for a 4-pound roast, total cooking time is 1 hour (60 minutes) at 325°F.
4. Increase the temperature to 500°F (260°C) and cook for an additional 15 minutes at the end.
5. Sprinkle with salt and fresh or dried rosemary needles after three-quarters of the cooking time.
6. Turn the roast using wooden spoons (ensure not to pierce the meat).
7. Season all over with salt, garlic salt, or seasoned salt, and freshly ground black pepper.
8. Swab the roast with fresh lemon juice for added flavor.
9. When cooked, remove the cords and slice the roast.
10. Strain the juices from the roasting pan, reserving some for reduction.
11. Add beef stock or red wine to the juices, minced shallots, and cook down into a sauce.
12. Adjust seasonings as desired and serve with the roast.
